mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation between unemployment spikes and recessions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of periods where UNRATE is rising, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid reduction in the federal funds rate indicates an aggressive monetary easing stance by the central bank, intended to stimulate economic activity by lowering borrowing costs and increasing liquidity, which typically supports equity markets.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops by more than 0.5 percentage points over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Central bank easing often follows economic weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return during triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales combined with consistent job creation signals strong consumer confidence and purchasing power, indicating healthy aggregate demand that directly translates into higher corporate revenues and supports S&P 500 valuations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the combined signal's Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of elevated inflation, sustained growth in industrial production indicates that supply is catching up with demand, which can alleviate price pressures and signal a more stable economic outlook, leading to positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances within the specified regime, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is not significantly positive in high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sudden decline in housing starts indicates a significant contraction in the construction sector and broader economic activity, often preceding or coinciding with economic downturns, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST drops by more than 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market often leads economic cycles
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 20d return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins, reduces real consumer purchasing power, and typically prompts central banks to implement tighter monetary policies, all of which negatively impact equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL is rising, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is tight (low unemployment), a rising federal funds rate signals that the central bank is actively trying to cool an overheating economy, which can lead to a slowdown in growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns as liquidity tightens.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 4.0% AND FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Tight labor markets can lead to wage inflation and monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the combined signal's Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of confirmed economic contraction (negative GDP growth), an unexpected uptick in retail sales can signal a nascent recovery in consumer demand, suggesting a potential bottoming out of the downturn and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales are a leading indicator of consumer sentiment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances within the specified regime, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is not significantly positive in contraction periods.
